card.intuition: dp[i][j] = minimum edits to convert s1[0..i-1] into s2[0..j-1]. If characters match, no edit needed: dp[i-1][j-1]. Otherwise, take 1 + the minimum of insert (dp[i][j-1]), delete (dp[i-1][j]), or replace (dp[i-1][j-1]).
card.analogy: Auto-correct: given a misspelled word, find the minimum number of character insertions, deletions, or substitutions to reach the intended word. The DP table encodes the cheapest transformation of every prefix pair.
card.space_complexity: O(m · n); O(min(m,n)) with two-row optimisation
gotchas: Base cases: dp[i][0] = i (delete all of s1 prefix), dp[0][j] = j (insert all of s2 prefix)
Three operations: insert into s1 (dp[i][j-1]+1), delete from s1 (dp[i-1][j]+1), substitute (dp[i-1][j-1]+1)
Damerau-Levenshtein also allows transpositions — one more case in the recurrence

exercise.prompt: Complete the edit distance DP recurrence.
hints: Characters match: no edit needed — dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j-1]
Substitute: cost 1 + dp[i-1][j-1] — replace the i-th char of s1 with the j-th char of s2
exercise.test_code:
assert edit_distance('horse', 'ros') == 3
assert edit_distance('intention', 'execution') == 5
assert edit_distance('', 'abc') == 3
assert edit_distance('abc', '') == 3
assert edit_distance('abc', 'abc') == 0
assert edit_distance('a', 'b') == 1
print('PASS')
